calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio appearing by CourtCall for Debra Bookman and Wesley Bookman (Co-Executors). Sil Liapis appearing in person for Angelique McGuire.

The Court stated before it is a Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account and for Allowance of Statutory Commissions, that it had read all the documents submitted, that it understands that there is a related trust, and that it understands Mr. Liapis' objections regarding this matter not being handled by way of a small estate affidavit. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that the estate was valued at $367,000.00 at the time of filing, that there were a number of bank accounts, that it took time to explore across several accounts, that it took time to obtain the information from the banks, that they didn't know the value, that they did do their due diligence, and that they would have opted to close probate had they known earlier. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio further stated that they were called into a court action, that it involved a clouded title of a piece of real property, that they tried to reduce the cost to the estate, that the matter went to a full trial, that they were dismissed from that matter in late 2019, that at that time there was a petition for final distribution, that they were called into court to address the delay, and that one of the executors was dealing with a terminal issue. Mr. Liapis stated that they failed to exercise due diligence before filing the probate. The Court addressed Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stating that the old judicial council form was used for notice and inquired if she was asking that the Court waive defective notice. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that was requesting that the Court waive the defective notice. The Court stated that the Co-Executors used estate funds to take care of the trust property and that they did it without a Court order. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio confirmed that the Co-Executors used estate funds to take care of the trust property, stated that they were confused as to what they should use the funds for, that they had been advised and had gone over the duties and liabilities, that in this case she was not consulted, and that this was the Co-Executors misperception. The Court stated that it hopes that the Co-Executors have a better understanding now. At the conclusion of argument, the Court took the Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account and for Allowance of Statutory Commissions under submission.

Judge: Joesph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-25-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-29-2020 Continued To Feb-25-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio for Debra Bookman and Wesley Bookman.
Sil Liapis for and with Angelique McGuire (Interested Party).

Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that the estate was thought to be the owner of a number of assets, that it was determined that it was not because most of the assets were "pay on death" clauses, that the estate was held up because it was named as a Defendant in a litigation matter with US Bank regarding a deed of trust, that they just received a dismissal of the action on November 21, 2019 and that they were waiting for that to close out the estate. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio also stated that one of the Co-Executors has been seriously ill and that they have everything ready, but they need the final bank statement. Mr. Liapis stated that he believes every asset was in the trust or were "pay on death" clauses and that he feels there is no need for an estate. Status on close of estate is continued to February 25, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio is to file either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration by February 18, 2020.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
